ticker_items: US-Israel war on Iran enters second week, costs $11.3 billion in six days
Iranian Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei injured in initial airstrikes
Israel strikes 10 Hezbollah targets in Beirut amid regional escalation
Iran threatens to target US-Israeli economic institutions across Middle East
Qatar PM calls for resilience as Saudi intercepts drone at oil field

middle_east.description: Israeli forces conducted precision strikes against 10 Hezbollah targets in Beirut.
Saudi Arabia intercepted Iranian drone headed toward Shaybah oil field.

middle_east.text: under no circumstances can we participate in the World Cup
middle_east.speaker: Ahmad Donyamali
middle_east.title: Iran's Minister of Sport and Youth

observers.description: Qatar's Prime Minister called for strengthening the country's resilience amid regional war. The nation is positioning itself to withstand hardship while maintaining regional stability.

Senator Nick McKim criticized Prime Minister Albanese for hitching Australia to the conflict by sending troops to the Middle East. Australia is being accused of helping the US 'rain down horror' on Iran.
Pretoria is conducting an awkward diplomatic dance between Washington and Tehran during the Iran war. South Africa is attempting to navigate competing pressures from both sides of the conflict.

financial.notes: Developed nations agreed to release record emergency oil reserves amid market pressure from US-Iran conflict, while Trump's Strategic Petroleum Reserve announcement stunned allies.

update_log.text: Iran threatens to target US and Israeli-affiliated banks and economic institutions across Middle East
Israeli forces strike 10 Hezbollah targets in Beirut as regional conflict expands
Saudi Arabia intercepts Iranian drone headed toward Shaybah oil field
Pentagon reports first 6 days of Iran war cost $11.3 billion
Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei reportedly injured in initial US-Israeli airstrikes
